entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 3
entries.excerpt: Having spoken of the nakedness of the first parents, which under their heavenly clothing was becoming and gave no occasion for shame, Moses turns to recount the cunning of the serpent and says: “Now the serpent was more subtle than any beast of the field which the Lord God had made” (Gen 3:1). The serpent was more cunning than the unreasoning animals over whom man ruled; but although it exceeded in cunning the level appointed to beasts, it does not follow that it rose to the level of man.…
